title: context_emotion evaluation_result
description: Output of evaluation/evaluate_candidate.py. Stored as evaluation_result.json inside both candidates/{version}/ and current/. compare_candidate.py reads exactly two of these (current + candidate) and never recomputes metrics itself.

onnx_sha256.description: sha256 of the exact model.onnx this evaluation ran against. deployment/model_store.py:validate_onnx_hash_consistency() refuses to package/promote if this doesn't match the onnx being shipped - without it, nothing stops a stale evaluation_result.json from riding along with an untested onnx file.

overall.description: Legitimate-user accuracy on the held-out eval set. Not the attacker-resistance signal - that's attacker_proxy below.

per_class.description: Keyed by emotion class name (must match common/constants.py EMOTION_CLASSES). Each value: {precision, recall, f1, support}.

human_ambiguity.description: Sourced from ops_metrics, not from the static training csv - see MLOPS_OPERATION_DESIGN.md '운영 데이터 vs 학습 데이터'. status='not_configured' until the ambiguous-answer-option UX (see retrospective discussion) ships and accumulates exposures.

attacker_proxy.description: status='not_configured' until an attacker proxy model + adversarial eval pool are chosen - see attacker_proxy_eval.py TODOs.
